title: hieracium wiesbaurianum subsp. nervulosum
intro: Hieracium wiesbaurianum subsp. nervulosum, also known as Wiesbaur's Hawkweed, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the dandelion family, native to Europe. This plant is used in herbal medicine due to its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. The medicinal properties of Wiesbaur's Hawkweed include its ability to reduce inflammation, promote wound healing, and treat various skin conditions, such as eczema and acne, which can provide health benefits like soothing skin irritations and enhancing the immune system. Herbal preparations of Hieracium wiesbaurianum subsp. nervulosum typically involve infusions or tinctures made from the plant's leaves and flowers, which can be used topically or internally to harness its medicinal properties.
